adjudication_raw:
Review: GNN fraud detection evaluation.

Verdict: empirical_test_agreed

The 15 pp fraud capture advantage (78% vs 63%) is compromised by: (1) GNN threshold validation-optimized for 0.8% while rules engine hits 0.8% due to seasonal volume accident — asymmetric optimization; (2) rules engine runs stale weights from 4 months before study, analyst revisions not replayed; (3) single-month evaluation insufficient for headcount decisions. The GNN may genuinely outperform an updated, properly-thresholded rules engine — that's the empirical question. Test: updated rule weights at deliberately-set 0.8% threshold on same test month.

empirical_test.measure: Fraud capture rate at 0.8% review rate on 10th-month test data
empirical_test.success_criterion: GNN leads updated rules engine (revised weights, 0.8% threshold by design) by ≥ 8 pp
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Gap below 5 pp with reconfigured rules engine
